title: setaria viridis subvar. linearis
intro: Setaria viridis subvar. linearis, commonly known as green foxtail or wild millet, is a subvariety of the Setaria viridis plant, a type of grass that grows in various parts of the world. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of green foxtail include its use as a natural diuretic, an antispasmodic, and an expectorant, making it useful for treating conditions such as urinary issues, muscle cramps, and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, green foxtail is often dried and made into tea or infusions, or used in tinctures and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
